# arb4j — Copilot Instructions

arb4j is a Java library for arbitrary-precision ball arithmetic, built on SWIG-generated JNI bindings to arblib/FLINT and an expression compiler (`arb.expressions`) that parses Unicode mathematical notation and emits JVM bytecode via ASM.

## Build & Test

```bash
# Build (libarblib.so is committed — no FLINT install needed)
LANG=en_US.UTF-8 LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 mvn install -Dmaven.test.skip=true

# Run all tests
mvn test

# Run a single test class or method
mvn test -Dtest=RealTest#testSqrt
mvn test -Dtest='RationalFunctionTest#testPowers'

# Run with expression-compiler tracing
mvn -Ptrace test -Dtest=ClassName#methodName
# (sets -Darb4j.trace=true -Darb4j.traceNodes=true -Dlogback.configurationFile=logback-debug.xml)

# Rebuild native lib — only when native/*.i SWIG files change
make && mvn install -Dmaven.test.skip=true

# Run JavaFX applications
bin/run arb.applications.Expressor   # AST viewer
bin/arbshell                         # JShell with arb.* preloaded
bin/symbolPalette
```

`LANG=en_US.UTF-8 LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8` is required — source files have Unicode names (`σField.java`, `RiemannζFunction.java`). `bin/run` reads `class.path` (generated by `mvn generate-sources`) and adds all `--add-modules`/`--add-opens` for JavaFX.

## Compiler Tracing Flags

Static booleans in `arb.expressions.Expression`, set via system properties:

| Property | Effect |
|---|---|
| `-Darb4j.saveClasses=true` | Write generated `.class` to `compiled/` |
| `-Darb4j.decompileClasses=true` | Decompile to `compiled/<name>.java` via vineflower |
| `-Darb4j.saveGraphs=true` | Dump GraphViz AST |
| `-Darb4j.trace=true` | Log every parse/compile step |
| `-Darb4j.traceNodes=true` | Log per-node bytecode emission |

To enable per-test, set `Expression.saveClasses = true` directly in Java before compilation.

## Package Structure

- **`arb.*`** — SWIG-generated native wrappers (`Real`, `Complex`, `Integer`, `RealPolynomial`, `ComplexMatrix`, etc.) plus hand-written extensions
- **`arb.expressions.*`** — expression compiler: `Expression`, `Context`, `Compiler`, `Parser`, `ExpressionClassLoader`, `FunctionMapping`, and AST node subpackages (`nodes/binary`, `nodes/unary`, `nodes/nary`)
- **`arb.functions.*`** — typed function interfaces and implementations by domain: `real/`, `complex/`, `integer/`, `rational/`, `algebraic/`, `polynomials/orthogonal/{real,complex}`
- **`arb.applications.*`** — JavaFX applications
- **`arb.expressions.nodes.*`** — each `Node` subclass owns `generate()` (ASM bytecode), `differentiate()` (symbolic), and `simplify()`

## Key Conventions

### `arb.*` types are `AutoCloseable` and own native memory
Always use try-with-resources or call `.close()`. This includes generated expression classes — they emit a `close()` that disposes all intermediate variables.

```java
try (Real x = new Real("25", 128); Real result = new Real()) {
    x.sqrt(128, result);
}
```

### Fluent last-argument-is-result pattern
The last argument to an arithmetic method is the result. If omitted, a new instance is returned.

```java
x.sqrt(128)          // allocates and returns new Real
x.sqrt(128, result)  // writes into result, returns result
x.sqrt(128, x)       // in-place (overwrites x)
```

### `Real`/`Complex`/`Integer`/`Magnitude` are already vectors — never use Java arrays
`Real.newVector(n)` creates an n-element vector; `x.get(i)` accesses element `i`; `x.dim()` returns the count. Java arrays of these types (`Real[]`, `Complex[]`) are wrong.

### Expression strings are literal source — never construct them programmatically
Strings passed to `.express(...)`, `.compile(...)`, `.declare(...)` are parsed as source code by the expression compiler. Concatenation, `String.format`, `replace`, `StringBuilder`, or any runtime construction of an expression string is forbidden. If a naming collision forces that temptation, fix the architecture instead.

### Compilation ordering rule
There is one rule: when `express` instantiates a generated class, every class that expression references must already be defined in the `ExpressionClassLoader`. Instantiation calls `injectVariableReferences` / `injectFunctionReferences`, which resolve every field type via reflection. There is no second pass.

- `compile(...)` — emits bytecode without instantiation. Call for every function that is referenced by others and must be defined first.
- `express(...)` — emits bytecode and instantiates. Call last, after all referenced classes are defined.

Single self-recursive sequences (`T` referencing `T`) need only `express` — the self-reference guard handles them automatically.

### Tests extend `TestCase` (JUnit 3-style) or use `@Test` (JUnit 4)
Most tests use `@Test`. The `junit.framework.TestCase` style is present but uncommon. Test sources mirror the main package layout under `src/test/java/arb/`.

### All source files carry a license javadoc reference
```java
/** @see BusinessSourceLicenseVersionOnePointOne © terms of the {@link TheArb4jLibrary} */
```

### Use literal Unicode — never `\uXXXX` escapes
Write `σ`, `➔`, `∑`, `∂` directly. Source files are UTF-8 throughout; Unicode escapes are unreadable and clash with the expression language where these characters are first-class identifiers.

### Import classes; never write fully-qualified names inline
`import arb.Integer;` then use `Integer`. Writing `arb.Integer` inline in code is wrong.
